_rationale: code_insight mode=git_analyze. INTENTIONAL DIVERGENCE: Go computes the git change report then DISCARDS it — its LCF formatter (formatStatisticsSection) never reads the GitAnalysis field, so Go emits an all-zero STATISTICS block. C++ surfaces the real data in a == GIT CHANGES == section (scope/files_changed/risk + top metrics issues), sourced from git::Analyzer (same engine as the git_analysis tool + HTTP /git-analyze). The two outputs share no common text, so content[].text is envelope-only here. Output also depends on the live working tree (staged changes), so it is not byte-stable across environments. Real-content correctness is locked C++-side by CodeInsightGitTest in tests/mcp_handlers_analysis_test.cpp against a controlled git fixture.

tiers._rationale_stable: JSON-RPC envelope + content type only. result.content[].text is intentional C++ enrichment Go discards (see _rationale) and is working-tree-dependent, so it is held in the ignore tier.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text: C++ emits real git change analysis (== GIT CHANGES ==) where Go's formatter drops the data and prints zero STATISTICS — no common subset to assert, and the content varies with the live working tree. Universal MCP descriptor noise (id/jsonrpc/meta) handled by the centralized baseline.
